mechanism_narrative: Higher monthly retail sales (RSAFS) indicate stronger consumer demand, which supports corporate earnings and forward equity returns over 20 trading days. This signal is always active and does not depend on specific thresholds.
failure_condition: If the Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS level and subsequent 20-day SPX return is not positive over any 3-year window, or if the Rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds effective rate rises above 0.25% from near-zero levels, it signals the start of monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism is triggered only when the rate crosses this threshold after a period of accommodation.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 0.25% after being below 0.25% for at least 6 months
failure_condition: If in the 12 months following such a trigger, the average 20-day SPX return is not negative or is statistically insignificant (p>0.10), or if Rank IC is <0.03 over all trigger events.

mechanism_narrative: When month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeds month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS), it suggests supply is outstripping demand, which leads to inventory accumulation and eventual production cuts, depressing corporate profits and S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. This signal is interaction-driven as it depends on the joint behavior of both series.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth > RSAFS MoM growth
failure_condition: If over all observed months when INDPRO MoM > RSAFS MoM, the 60-day forward SPX return is not negative with statistical significance (p<0.05), or if the Rank IC for the signal is below |0.03|.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime (UNRATE above 5.5%), a month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals strengthening labor market conditions, which boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. This mechanism is conditional on the regime of elevated unemployment; in a low-unemployment regime the relationship may reverse.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE decreases from previous month while UNRATE > 5.5%
failure_condition: If in months where UNRATE > 5.5% and UNRATE declines MoM, the 20-day forward SPX return is not positive with p<0.05, or if the signal's Rank IC in this regime is below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher monthly housing starts (HOUST) signal robust construction activity and consumer demand for housing, which supports economic growth and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. This signal is always active across all observed values.
failure_condition: If the Spearman rank correlation between HOUST and subsequent 5-day SPX return is not positive over any 3-year rolling window, or if Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When month-over-month industrial production (INDPRO) growth exceeds 0.5%, it signals a strong expansion in manufacturing output, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is only active when the threshold is crossed; below that, the signal is neutral or negative.
failure_condition: If over all months with INDPRO MoM > 0.5%, the 60-day forward SPX return is not positive with statistical significance (p<0.05), or if Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the year-over-year growth of real GDP (GDPC1) is lower than the year-over-year growth of industrial production (INDPRO), it suggests an imbalance where production is outpacing final demand, leading to inventory buildup and eventual economic slowdown, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. This signal requires joint observation of both series.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 YoY growth < INDPRO YoY growth
failure_condition: If over all quarters when GDPC1 YoY < INDPRO YoY (using latest available data), the 60-day forward SPX return is not negative with p<0.05, or if Rank IC < |0.03|.

mechanism_narrative: Higher levels of total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) reflect a strong labor market and growing aggregate income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This signal is always active and does not require a threshold.
failure_condition: If the Spearman rank correlation between PAYEMS level and subsequent 20-day SPX return is not positive over any 3-year window, or Rank IC < 0.02.
